_api/CalloraVoipSdk.BridgeAudioFormat.ht.title: Enum BridgeAudioFormat | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.BridgeAudioFormat.ht.summary: Enum BridgeAudioFormat Namespace CalloraVoipSdk Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll Audio format delivered to and expected from a media consumer (the "bridge"/tap), such as an AI realtime bridge. When it differs from the negotiated wire codec, the SDK transcodes audio transparently so the consumer sees a single, fixed codec regardless of what the SIP peer negotiated. public enum BridgeAudioFormat Fields Passthrough = 0 Deliver and send the raw negotiated wire payload unchanged (default). The consumer must handle whatever codec was negotiated. Pcmu = 1 Always deliver and accept G.711 µ-law (PCMU, 8 kHz). Inbound wire audio is transcoded to µ-law and outbound µ-law is transcoded to the wire codec. Supported wire codecs: Opus and G.711 (µ-law/A-law); other codecs are delivered as passthrough with a warning.

RecommendedBitrateBps { get; } Property Value long? Methods AttachToCall(ICall) Attaches this sender to one call. Stops firing on Detach()/dispose. Typical usage: sender.KeyFrameRequested += (_, _) => encoder.ForceKeyFrame(); event EventHandler? KeyFrameRequested Event Type EventHandler RecommendedBitrateChanged Raised when the SDK's recommendation for the attached call changes — subscribe and adjust your encoder's target bitrate. Stops firing on Detach()/dispose. The typical usage is the whole point of the API: sender.RecommendedBitrateChanged += (_, e) => encoder.SetBitrate(e.RecommendedBitrateBps); event EventHandler<VideoBitrateRecommendationEventArgs>? _api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Med.title: Struct VideoFrame | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Med.summary: Struct VideoFrame Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll One encoded video frame on the public media contract. The SDK is transport-only: the payload carries already-encoded codec bytes (one coded picture) as negotiated for the call — it is not decoded pixels, and the SDK never encodes or decodes. Bring your own codec (VP8, H.264, …) and read PayloadType to identify it. public readonly record struct VideoFrame : IEquatable<VideoFrame> Implements IEquatable<VideoFrame> Inherited Members ValueType.Equals(object) ValueType.GetHashCode() ValueType.ToString() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors VideoFrame(ReadOnlyMemory<byte>, int, uint, bool) One encoded video frame on the public media contract. The SDK is transport-only: the payload carries already-encoded codec bytes (one coded picture) as negotiated for the call — it is not decoded pixels, and the SDK never encodes or decodes. Bring your own codec (VP8, H.264, …) and read PayloadType to identify it. public VideoFrame(ReadOnlyMemory<byte> Payload, int PayloadType, uint RtpTimestamp, bool IsKeyFrame) Parameters Payload ReadOnlyMemory<byte> Encoded video frame bytes (one coded picture). PayloadType int Negotiated RTP payload type identifying the video codec. RtpTimestamp uint The frame's RTP timestamp on the video clock (90 kHz). All RTP packets carrying this frame share this value; it is absolute, not a duration. IsKeyFrame bool true when the frame is (or is known to be) an intra-coded key frame decodable without prior frames; false for a delta frame or when the type is not known. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException No call is attached. ObjectDisposedException The sender has been disposed. Events KeyFrameRequested Raised when the remote peer requested a keyframe (RTCP PLI/FIR) for the attached call — force your encoder to emit an intra frame next and send it via SendAsync(VideoFrame, CancellationToken) with IsKeyFrame set. Stops firing on Detach()/dispose. Typical usage: sender.KeyFrameRequested += (_, _) => encoder.ForceKeyFrame(); public event EventHandler? KeyFrameRequested Event Type EventHandler RecommendedBitrateChanged Raised when the SDK's recommendation for the attached call changes — subscribe and adjust your encoder's target bitrate. Stops firing on Detach()/dispose. The typical usage is the whole point of the API: sender.RecommendedBitrateChanged += (_, e) => encoder.SetBitrate(e.RecommendedBitrateBps); public event EventHandler<VideoBitrateRecommendationEventArgs>? RecommendedBitrateChanged Event Type EventHandler<VideoBitrateRecommendationEventArgs>

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Med.title: Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Med.summary: Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Application.Media Classes MediaConnector Connects media receivers to media senders with bounded in-memory backpressure. MediaFrameReceivedEventArgs Event payload for FrameReceived. Delivered synchronously on the media path — handlers must return quickly (see the event's documentation). MediaManager Factory and orchestration entrypoint for media routing, recording and playback. MediaReceiver Surfaces a call's inbound (decoded) audio frames to the application via the FrameReceived event. Attach to a call to start receiving; a faulting subscriber is isolated so it neither stops other subscribers nor the RTP callback. MediaSender Feeds application-supplied audio frames into a call's outbound RTP stream. Attach to a call, then push frames with SendAsync(MediaFrame, CancellationToken). Frames sent while the call is not connected/on-hold are silently dropped rather than throwing. MediaSessionErrorEventArgs Error payload raised by recording and playback sessions. MediaSessionStateChangedEventArgs Describes one media session state transition. PlaybackOptions Runtime options for playback sessions. PlaybackRequest Input payload for starting a playback session. RecordingOptions Configuration for call/conference recording sessions. VideoBitrateRecommendationEventArgs Event payload for RecommendedBitrateChanged: the SDK's ready-to-use outbound video bitrate recommendation and the coarse network quality behind it. Set your encoder's target bitrate to RecommendedBitrateBps — the SDK never encodes (transport-only). VideoFrameReceivedEventArgs Event payload for FrameReceived. Delivered synchronously on the media path — handlers must return quickly (see the event's documentation). VideoReceiver Surfaces a call's inbound encoded video frames to the application via the FrameReceived event. Attach to a call to start receiving; a faulting subscriber is isolated so it neither stops other subscribers nor the RTP callback. Mirrors MediaReceiver for video — transport-only, so the payload is encoded, not decoded. VideoSender Feeds application-supplied encoded video frames into a call's outbound RTP stream. Attach to a call, then push frames with SendAsync(VideoFrame, CancellationToken). Frames sent while the call is not connected/on-hold are silently dropped rather than throwing. Mirrors MediaSender for video — transport-only, so the payload must already be encoded. Structs MediaFrame One encoded audio frame on the public media contract. The payload carries codec data as negotiated for the call (identified by PayloadType, e.g. 0 = PCMU, 8 = PCMA, 9 = G.722); it is not decoded PCM. VideoFrame One encoded video frame on the public media contract. The SDK is transport-only: the payload carries already-encoded codec bytes (one coded picture) as negotiated for the call — it is not decoded pixels, and the SDK never encodes or decodes. Bring your own codec (VP8, H.264, …) and read PayloadType to identify it. Interfaces IMediaManager Factory and orchestration entrypoint for media routing, recording and playback. IMediaReceiver Public per-call tap for inbound media frames. Multiple receivers can be attached to the same call in parallel (e.g. default audio, recording and a realtime bridge); every attached receiver observes every inbound frame. IMediaSender Public per-call injection point for outbound media frames. Frames are handed to the call's active send path; the payload must already be encoded in the negotiated codec (see MediaParameters for payload type and clock rate). IMixedMediaBus Abstraction for a mixed PCM16 media bus that supports recording taps and playback injection. IPlaybackSession Public handle for one active playback session. IRecordingEncryptionProvider Optional provider that encrypts finalized recording files. IRecordingSession Public handle for one active recording session. IVideoReceiver Public per-call tap for inbound encoded video frames. Multiple receivers can attach to the same call in parallel; every attached receiver observes every reassembled inbound frame. The SDK is transport-only — decode the payload with your own codec. IVideoSender Public per-call injection point for outbound encoded video frames. Frames are handed to the call's active video send path; the payload must already be encoded in the negotiated codec (see MediaParameters for the negotiated media). The SDK is transport-only and never encodes. Enums AudioFileFormat Audio file container format used by recording and playback sessions. MediaSessionState Runtime state for recording/playback media sessions.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.title: Class CallIceSnapshot | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.summary: Class CallIceSnapshot Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Read-only snapshot of ICE (RFC 8445 / RFC 7675) connectivity establishment for one call media leg. Produced once candidate-pair selection completes and exposed via IceSnapshot. It explains which local/remote candidate pair media flows over and why — otherwise an ICE-driven media path or teardown is opaque to the consumer. _api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.title: Enum CallIceState | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.summary: Enum CallIceState Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Public lifecycle state of ICE (RFC 8445) connectivity establishment for one call media leg, surfaced read-only via State. Non-ICE calls never produce an ICE snapshot, so this state is only meaningful when ICE was negotiated for the leg. public enum CallIceState Fields Checking = 3 Connectivity checks are running. Connected = 5 A candidate pair was selected successfully; media flows over the selected pair. Disabled = 0 ICE is disabled or no ICE metadata was negotiated for this leg. Disconnected = 6 The selected pair lost connectivity after Connected — RFC 7675 consent was not refreshed in time and media transmission ceased. Transient: the path may recover (or be revived by an ICE restart), so the socket stays open. Distinct from Failed, which is an establishment failure before any pair was selected. Failed = 7 ICE checks failed; the leg fell back to the negotiated SDP endpoints. Gathered = 2 Candidate gathering completed. Gathering = 1 Local candidate gathering is in progress. Nominating = 4 A successful pair is being nominated.

LocalSsrc uint Our synchronization source identifier (RFC 3550 §5.1). RemoteSsrc uint? The peer's synchronization source identifier once at least one RTP/RTCP packet has been received from it; null before the remote SSRC is known. PacketsSent uint Total RTP packets we have sent on this leg (RTCP SR sender count). OctetsSent uint Total RTP payload octets we have sent on this leg (RTCP SR octet count). PacketsReceived uint Total RTP packets received from the peer on this leg. PacketsExpected uint Number of RTP packets expected from the peer based on the observed sequence-number range (RFC 3550 §A.3); PacketsExpected - PacketsReceived approximates loss. CumulativePacketsLost int Cumulative number of RTP packets from the peer lost since the start of reception (RFC 3550 §6.4.1); may be negative when duplicates arrive. FractionLost byte The 8-bit fixed-point loss fraction over the last reporting interval (RFC 3550 §6.4.1): the loss ratio is FractionLost / 256.0. ExtendedHighestSequenceNumber uint Extended highest RTP sequence number received from the peer (RFC 3550 §6.4.1): the highest 16-bit sequence number combined with the wrap-around count in the upper bits. InterarrivalJitterRtpUnits uint Interarrival jitter estimate in RTP timestamp units (RFC 3550 §6.4.1). Divide by the codec clock rate to convert to seconds. _api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.title: Enum CallState | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ca.summary: Enum CallState Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Lifecycle state of a call. public enum CallState Fields Connected = 3 The call is answered and media is (or is about to be) flowing. Dialing = 1 Outbound call: the INVITE has been sent and the SDK is awaiting a response. Idle = 0 No call activity; the initial state before dialing or ringing. OnHold = 4 The call is on hold; media is suspended in at least one direction. Ringing = 2 The call is ringing — outbound (remote alerting) or inbound (awaiting local accept/reject). Terminated = 6 The call has ended (hung up, rejected, or failed); a terminal state. Transferring = 5 A transfer (blind or attended) is in progress for this call.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.IC.title: Interface ICall | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.IC.summary: Interface ICall Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll A single call and its lifecycle. public interface ICall Remarks Event threading contract. Event handlers run synchronously on the SDK thread that raised the event — this is usually not the thread that created the call. Handlers therefore must not block or perform long-running/synchronous I/O: a blocked handler stalls the SDK path that raised it (SIP signaling or media), delaying every other call on the same line. Off-load real work to your own task or queue, e.g. call.StateChanged += (_, e) => _ = Task.Run(() => Handle(e));. Handlers should also not throw. See each event for the specific thread and whether it is serialized. TransferRequested is the exception to off-loading: it needs a synchronous accept/reject decision, so make that decision quickly inline (do not await long work). Error contract. The action methods split into two error styles by return type. The core lifecycle operations return Task (or Task<TResult> of bool for AttendedTransferAsync(ICall, CancellationToken)) and throw: invalid usage surfaces as InvalidOperationException (wrong state or direction), ArgumentException (invalid argument), and cancellation surfaces as OperationCanceledException; transport, timeout and unexpected transport-layer failures propagate as-is. The extended in-dialog / inbound-response operations return CallActionResult instead and do not throw for foreseeable outcomes: an invalid state or direction, a protocol rejection (for example a SIP 4xx from the peer), an invalid request, cancellation, and transport failures are all reported as a non-success CallActionResult whose Status classifies the cause (see CallActionStatus). These result-returning methods are the ones that involve a SIP round-trip whose negative answer is a normal protocol result rather than a programming error. Per-method tags below state the exact exceptions and result semantics. Properties CallId Stable identifier of this call within the SDK. CallId CallId { get; } Property Value CallId Direction Whether the call was placed locally (Outbound) or received (Inbound). CallDirection Direction { get; } Property Value CallDirection Diversion First Diversion URI (RFC 5806) from an inbound INVITE describing where the call was diverted from; null for outbound calls or when no Diversion header is present. Informational routing history, surfaced as-received. string? Diversion { get; } Property Value string EarlyMediaSdp SDP body carried on a provisional (180/183) response — the early-media description offered by the far end before the call is answered (RFC 3960), separate from the final answer in MediaParameters. null until such a provisional arrives. Default implementation returns null. string? EarlyMediaSdp { get; } Property Value string IceConnectionState Running ICE transport state for this leg (RFC 8445 / RFC 7675). Unlike the one-shot IceSnapshot (final establishment state), this tracks post-establishment changes — notably Connected → Disconnected when consent is lost. Disabled for non-ICE legs. Changes are signalled by IceConnectionStateChanged. CallIceState IceConnectionState { get; } Property Value CallIceState IceSnapshot Read-only ICE (RFC 8445) connectivity snapshot for this leg once candidate-pair selection completes, or null for calls where ICE was not negotiated. Reports the final ICE state and the selected local/remote candidate pair. CallIceSnapshot? IceSnapshot { get; } Property Value CallIceSnapshot Line The phone line this call belongs to. IPhoneLine Line { get; } Property Value IPhoneLine MediaParameters Negotiated media parameters (codec, endpoints). Set once Connected is reached; null before that. CallMediaParameters? MediaParameters { get; } Property Value CallMediaParameters QualitySnapshot Latest quality snapshot derived from RTP/RTCP runtime metrics. Before media starts, this value is an empty baseline snapshot. CallQualitySnapshot QualitySnapshot { get; } Property Value CallQualitySnapshot RemoteAssertedIdentity Peer-asserted caller identity (P-Asserted-Identity, RFC 3325) parsed from an inbound INVITE when the sending peer is trusted, for trunk/PBX routing; null for outbound calls, untrusted peers, or when the header is absent. string? RemoteAssertedIdentity { get; } Property Value string RemoteParty The remote party's SIP URI or address-of-record. string RemoteParty { get; } Property Value string RtpStatistics Latest raw RTP transport statistics (SSRC identifiers, packet/octet counters, RFC 3550 loss and jitter counters) for this leg, or null until the first RTCP reporting interval has produced counters. Complements QualitySnapshot with the underlying uninterpreted counters for diagnostics and billing. CallRtpStatistics? RtpStatistics { get; } Property Value CallRtpStatistics? StartedAt UTC timestamp of when this call object was created (dial or inbound INVITE). DateTimeOffset StartedAt { get; } Property Value DateTimeOffset State Current lifecycle state; changes are signalled by StateChanged. CallState State { get; } Property Value CallState Methods AcceptAsync(CancellationToken) Accepts an inbound ringing call and moves it to Connected. Task AcceptAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancels the accept; on cancellation OperationCanceledException is thrown. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException The call is not Inbound, or its state is not Ringing. OperationCanceledException ct was canceled. AttendedTransferAsync(ICall, CancellationToken) Attended transfer: transfer this call to the party in consultationCall. Task<bool> AttendedTransferAsync(ICall consultationCall, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters consultationCall ICall The consultation call to transfer to. Must be a call created by this SDK. ct CancellationToken Cancels the transfer; on cancellation OperationCanceledException is thrown. Returns Task<bool> true when the transfer completed and this call moved to Terminated; false when the transfer did not complete and this call returned to Connected. Exceptions ArgumentException consultationCall is not a call created by this SDK. OperationCanceledException ct was canceled. BlindTransferAsync(string, CancellationToken) Performs a blind transfer to targetUri. On a successful transfer the call moves to Terminated; if the transfer fails it returns to Connected. Either way the task completes without a return value. Task BlindTransferAsync(string targetUri, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters targetUri string The transfer target URI. ct CancellationToken Cancels the transfer; on cancellation OperationCanceledException is thrown. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException The call state is not Connected. OperationCanceledException ct was canceled. HangupAsync(CancellationToken) Hangs up the call and transitions it to Terminated. If the call is already Terminated this is a no-op that completes successfully. Task HangupAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Accepted for signature symmetry but currently not forwarded to the transport, so the hangup is not cancelled via this token. Returns Task HoldAsync(CancellationToken) Places the active call on hold and emits a local HoldStateChanged event. Task HoldAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Accepted for signature symmetry but currently not forwarded to the transport, so the hold is not cancelled via this token. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException The call state is not Connected. RedirectAsync(IReadOnlyList<string>, int, CancellationToken) Redirects an inbound ringing call with a 3xx SIP response and contact targets. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead. Task<CallActionResult> RedirectAsync(IReadOnlyList<string> contactUris, int statusCode = 302, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters contactUris IReadOnlyList<string> The redirect contact target URIs. statusCode int The 3xx SIP status code to respond with (default 302 Moved Temporarily). ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded once the redirect is sent and the call is terminated; InvalidState when the call is not Inbound or its state is not Ringing (checked before any SIP round-trip); Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. RejectAsync(int, string?, CancellationToken) Rejects an inbound ringing call with a 4xx/5xx/6xx SIP status. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead (see the interface error contract). Task<CallActionResult> RejectAsync(int statusCode = 486, string? reasonPhrase = null, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters statusCode int The SIP status code to reject with (default 486 Busy Here). reasonPhrase string Optional SIP reason phrase; a default is derived when omitted. ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded once the reject is sent and the call is terminated; InvalidState when the call is not Inbound or its state is not Ringing (checked before any SIP round-trip); Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. SendDtmfAsync(DtmfTone, CancellationToken) Sends one DTMF tone while the call is connected. Task SendDtmfAsync(DtmfTone tone, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters tone DtmfTone The tone to send. ct CancellationToken Accepted for signature symmetry but currently not forwarded to the transport, so the send is not cancelled via this token. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException The call state is not Connected. SendInfoAsync(string, string, CancellationToken) Sends in-dialog SIP INFO. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead. Task<CallActionResult> SendInfoAsync(string contentType, string body, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters contentType string The INFO body content type. body string The INFO message body. ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded once the INFO is sent; Canceled on cancellation; InvalidRequest for an invalid argument; InvalidState when the transport reports the operation invalid for the current state; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. SendNotifyAsync(string, string, string?, string?, CancellationToken) Sends in-dialog SIP NOTIFY. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead. Task<CallActionResult> SendNotifyAsync(string eventType, string subscriptionState, string? contentType = null, string? body = null, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters eventType string The SIP event package the notification belongs to. subscriptionState string The Subscription-State header value. contentType string Optional body content type. body string Optional request body. ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded when the peer accepts; Rejected when the peer declines the NOTIFY; Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. SendOptionsAsync(CancellationToken) Sends in-dialog SIP OPTIONS. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead. Task<CallActionResult> SendOptionsAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded when the peer accepts; Rejected when the peer declines the OPTIONS; Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. SendSubscribeAsync(string, int, string?, string?, CancellationToken) Sends in-dialog SIP SUBSCRIBE. Does not throw for foreseeable outcomes; the result carries the classification instead. Task<CallActionResult> SendSubscribeAsync(string eventType, int expiresSeconds = 300, string? acceptHeader = null, string? body = null, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters eventType string The SIP event package to subscribe to. expiresSeconds int Requested subscription duration in seconds (default 300). acceptHeader string Optional Accept header value. body string Optional request body. ct CancellationToken Cancels the operation; reported as Canceled, not thrown. Returns Task<CallActionResult> Succeeded when the peer accepts; Rejected when the peer declines the SUBSCRIBE; Canceled on cancellation; or Failed on a transport-layer failure. UnholdAsync(CancellationToken) Takes the call off hold and emits a local HoldStateChanged event. Task UnholdAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Accepted for signature symmetry but currently not forwarded to the transport, so the unhold is not cancelled via this token. Returns Task Exceptions InvalidOperationException The call state is not OnHold. Events DtmfReceived Raised when a DTMF tone is received. This event may fire from two threads: the SIP signaling thread (SIP INFO tones) and the media receive thread (RFC 4733 in-band RTP events). It is therefore not guaranteed single-threaded — keep the handler thread-safe and fast. event EventHandler<DtmfReceivedEventArgs>? DtmfReceived Event Type EventHandler<DtmfReceivedEventArgs> HoldStateChanged Raised when the remote party puts the call on or off hold. Serialized on the SIP signaling thread; buffered and replayed like StateChanged when no handler is attached. event EventHandler<HoldStateChangedEventArgs>? HoldStateChanged Event Type EventHandler<HoldStateChangedEventArgs> IceConnectionStateChanged Raised when the running ICE transport state changes after establishment (for example Connected → Disconnected on consent loss). Fires on a media/RTCP thread, not the signaling thread. Lets the application react to a lost media path — tear down, alert the user, or (later) trigger an ICE restart. Not buffered. event EventHandler<CallIceConnectionStateChangedEventArgs>? IceConnectionStateChanged Event Type EventHandler<CallIceConnectionStateChangedEventArgs> QualitySnapshotChanged Raised periodically with an updated call-quality snapshot. Fires on a media/RTCP thread (RTCP send-timer and receive driven, not the signaling thread), so it may interleave with the signaling-thread events above. Not buffered. event EventHandler<CallQualitySnapshotChangedEventArgs>? QualitySnapshotChanged Event Type EventHandler<CallQualitySnapshotChangedEventArgs> StateChanged Raised when State changes. Serialized on the SIP signaling thread. If no handler is attached yet, state changes are buffered and replayed in order once one is — the initial state is never missed. See the interface remarks for the handler contract. event EventHandler<CallStateChangedEventArgs>? StateChanged Event Type EventHandler<CallStateChangedEventArgs> TransferRequested Raised when the peer requests a transfer (SIP REFER), on the SIP signaling thread. The handler must set the accept/reject decision on the event args synchronously (see remarks); it drives the SIP response, so decide quickly and do not await long-running work. event EventHandler<TransferRequestedEventArgs>? TransferRequested Event Type EventHandler<TransferRequestedEventArgs>

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ne.title: Enum NetworkQuality | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.Ne.summary: Enum NetworkQuality Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Coarse, ready-to-use indicator of the media path's health, derived by the SDK from the congestion signal (delay trend + loss). Meant for a simple UI hint or a plugin decision — read it instead of interpreting raw metrics yourself. A call-media value describing the leg, alongside CallQualitySnapshot. public enum NetworkQuality Fields Fair = 1 The path shows mild stress: some loss or a rising delay trend, but still usable. Good = 0 The path has headroom: delay is stable and loss is negligible. Poor = 2 The path is congested: delay is overusing or loss is high — reduce the send bitrate.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.ht.title: Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls.ht.summary: Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Calls Classes CallActionResult Unified result model for extended call control operations. CallIceCandidate Represents one ICE candidate associated with a call media leg. CallIceSnapshot Read-only snapshot of ICE (RFC 8445 / RFC 7675) connectivity establishment for one call media leg. Produced once candidate-pair selection completes and exposed via IceSnapshot. It explains which local/remote candidate pair media flows over and why — otherwise an ICE-driven media path or teardown is opaque to the consumer. Non-ICE calls never produce a snapshot (IceSnapshot stays null). CallMediaParameters Negotiated media parameters for one active call leg. Produced by the infrastructure adapter after SDP offer/answer exchange and consumed by the application media orchestrator to set up RTP I/O. A record so a derived leg (e.g. after ICE candidate-pair selection) can be produced with a with expression that carries every field across — a hand-written copy had silently dropped the SDES/DTLS key material and video parameters when overriding the endpoints. CallVideoParameters Negotiated video parameters for one call leg (WebRTC phase 2). Present on Video only when the SDP exchange negotiated an active video m-line with a codec the SDK supports; null keeps the call audio-only. Grouped into one object so parameter enrichment passes video through as a unit. DialOptions Per-call dialing options — a pure Domain value object supplied to DialAsync(string, DialOptions?, CancellationToken). Structs CallId Strongly-typed, immutable identifier for a single call instance. CallQualitySnapshot Public quality snapshot for one call media leg. Values are updated continuously while the call is active and can be used by SDK consumers for routing, alerting, or UI quality indicators. CallRtpStatistics Raw RTP transport statistics for one active call media leg, exposed for diagnostics, billing and troubleshooting. Unlike CallQualitySnapshot — which surfaces derived quality metrics (jitter/loss in ms/%, MOS) — this record carries the underlying RFC 3550 counters (SSRC identifiers, packet/octet counts, RTP-unit jitter) without interpretation. Available on RtpStatistics once media is flowing. DtmfTone Validated DTMF tone (0-9, *, #, A-D). Interfaces ICall A single call and its lifecycle. Enums CallActionStatus Outcome category for extended call control operations. CallDirection Indicates whether a call was initiated by the local party or received from a remote party. CallIceState Public lifecycle state of ICE (RFC 8445) connectivity establishment for one call media leg, surfaced read-only via State. Non-ICE calls never produce an ICE snapshot, so this state is only meaningful when ICE was negotiated for the leg. CallState Lifecycle state of a call. NetworkQuality Coarse, ready-to-use indicator of the media path's health, derived by the SDK from the congestion signal (delay trend + loss). Meant for a simple UI hint or a plugin decision — read it instead of interpreting raw metrics yourself. A call-media value describing the leg, alongside CallQualitySnapshot.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.IP.title: Interface IPhoneLine | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.IP.summary: Interface IPhoneLine Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll A registered SIP line (account) that places and receives calls. public interface IPhoneLine Remarks All events on this interface are raised on the SDK's SIP signaling/registration thread and run the handler synchronously on it. Handlers must not block or throw — a blocked handler stalls signaling and registration for the line. Off-load real work to your own task; see ICall remarks for the same contract and an example. Properties Account The SIP account this line registers and calls with. SipAccount Account { get; } Property Value SipAccount LineId Stable identifier of this line within the SDK. LineId LineId { get; } Property Value LineId State Current registration state; changes are signalled by StateChanged. LineState State { get; } Property Value LineState Methods DialAsync(string, DialOptions?, CancellationToken) Places an outbound call from this line to targetUri and returns the new call already in Dialing. Track progress via the returned call's StateChanged event. Task<ICall> DialAsync(string targetUri, DialOptions? options = null, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters targetUri string Destination SIP URI or number to dial. options DialOptions Per-call options; null uses Default. ct CancellationToken Cancels the dial attempt. Returns Task<ICall> The newly created outbound call. SendMessageAsync(string, string, string, CancellationToken) Sends an out-of-dialog SIP MESSAGE (RFC 3428 pager-mode instant message) from this line. Task SendMessageAsync(string targetUri, string body, string contentType = "text/plain", CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters targetUri string The recipient's SIP URI. body string The message text/body. contentType string The body's MIME type; defaults to text/plain. ct CancellationToken Cancels the send. Returns Task A task that completes when the peer answers 2xx; it faults on a non-2xx or no response. UnregisterAsync(CancellationToken) Unregisters this line (sends REGISTER with Expires: 0) and stops automatic re-registration. Task UnregisterAsync(CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters ct CancellationToken Cancels the unregister request. Returns Task Events IncomingCall Raised when an inbound call arrives (signaling thread). The call is already ringing when this fires; accept or reject it via the call on the event args. Keep the handler fast (see remarks). event EventHandler<IncomingCallEventArgs>? IncomingCall Event Type EventHandler<IncomingCallEventArgs> IncomingMessage Raised when an inbound out-of-dialog SIP MESSAGE (RFC 3428 pager-mode IM) arrives for this line (signaling thread). The SDK has already answered it 200 OK; no reply is required. Keep the handler fast (see remarks). event EventHandler<IncomingMessageEventArgs>? IncomingMessage Event Type EventHandler<IncomingMessageEventArgs> LineReconnectFailed Raised when the line permanently fails to re-register and enters Failed. No further reconnect attempts will be made. event EventHandler<LineReconnectFailedEventArgs>? LineReconnectFailed Event Type EventHandler<LineReconnectFailedEventArgs> LineReconnecting Raised each time the SDK begins a reconnect attempt after losing the SIP registration. The line is already in Reconnecting when this event fires. event EventHandler<LineReconnectingEventArgs>? LineReconnecting Event Type EventHandler<LineReconnectingEventArgs> StateChanged Raised when the line's registration State changes (signaling thread). event EventHandler<LineStateChangedEventArgs>? _api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Li.title: Enum LineState | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Li.summary: Enum LineState Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Describes the current registration state of a phone line. public enum LineState Fields Failed = 5 Re-registration permanently failed — either MaxRetries was exceeded or the server rejected credentials (401/403). No further attempts will be made. Reconnecting = 3 A previous registration was lost and the SDK is waiting to retry. Transitions back to Registering before each attempt. Registered = 2 Successfully registered with the SIP registrar. Registering = 1 A registration request is in flight. RegistrationFailed = 4 The most recent registration attempt failed; retry may follow. Unregistered = 0 Not registered; no active SIP binding.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Re.title: Enum ReregisterFailReason | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Re.summary: Enum ReregisterFailReason Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Describes why a phone line permanently failed to re-register and entered Failed. public enum ReregisterFailReason Fields AuthenticationFailed = 1 The SIP server rejected the credentials with a 401 or 403 response. Retrying with the same credentials would not succeed. MaxRetriesExceeded = 0 The maximum number of reconnect attempts configured in MaxRetries was exceeded without a successful registration.

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Re.title: Class ReregisterOptions | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Re.summary: Class ReregisterOptions Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Controls automatic re-registration behavior when a SIP registration is lost due to a network disruption or server restart. public sealed class ReregisterOptions Inheritance object ReregisterOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields Default Default options: auto-reregister enabled, unlimited retries, exponential backoff 2 s – 60 s. public static readonly ReregisterOptions Default Field Value ReregisterOptions Disabled Disabled options: no automatic re-registration; line transitions directly to RegistrationFailed on disruption. public static readonly ReregisterOptions Disabled Field Value ReregisterOptions Properties AutoReregister When true the SDK automatically attempts to re-register after a connection disruption. Default: true. public bool AutoReregister { get; init; } Property Value bool InitialRetryDelay Delay before the first reconnect attempt and base for exponential backoff. Default: 2 seconds. public TimeSpan InitialRetryDelay { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan MaxCorrectiveReregistrations Maximum consecutive corrective re-registrations triggered by a changed NAT-learned public contact within one cycle, before the line settles on the current address. Caps a re-register storm on a pathological NAT that reflects a new port on every REGISTER. Default: 3. public int MaxCorrectiveReregistrations { get; init; } Property Value int MaxRetries Maximum number of reconnect attempts before the line permanently transitions to Failed. A value of 0 means unlimited retries. Default: 0. public int MaxRetries { get; init; } Property Value int MaxRetryDelay Upper bound on the delay between reconnect attempts (caps exponential growth). Default: 60 seconds. public TimeSpan MaxRetryDelay { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan MinRefreshInterval Lower bound on the refresh interval, guarding against REGISTER churn when a registrar reports an implausibly short lifetime. Never applied above the binding lifetime itself. Default: 15 seconds. public TimeSpan MinRefreshInterval { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan RefreshRatio Fraction of the granted registration lifetime at which the binding is refreshed (0 < ratio < 1). Default: 0.8 (refresh at 80% of the lifetime). public double RefreshRatio { get; init; } Property Value double

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Si.title: Class SipAccount | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines.Si.summary: Class SipAccount Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Lines Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Configuration of a SIP account used to register a IPhoneLine and place/receive calls. public sealed class SipAccount Inheritance object SipAccount Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AcceptTrunkInbound Whether the line accepts inbound INVITEs delivered by its registrar/proxy peer or, only when the source is unknown, addressed to its registered domain (SIP-trunk behavior). When true (default) a call for the exact username is always accepted, plus — when no InboundNumbers whitelist is set — calls from the trusted registrar peer and domain-addressed calls from an unknown source. Set to false for a strict 1:1 user account that must accept only its own username. Ignored when InboundNumbers is set (whitelist wins). public bool AcceptTrunkInbound { get; init; } Property Value bool Address The account's SIP address-of-record, derived as sip:Username@SipServer. public SipAddress Address { get; } Property Value SipAddress Credentials The authentication credentials derived from Username and Password. public SipCredentials Credentials { get; } Property Value SipCredentials DisplayName Human-readable caller name shown to the remote party; empty by default. public string DisplayName { get; init; } Property Value string EffectivePort The port actually used: Port when non-zero, otherwise the default for Transport (5060 UDP/TCP, 5061 TLS, 80 WS, 443 WSS). public int EffectivePort { get; } Property Value int InboundNumbers Optional inbound number (DID) whitelist for SIP trunks. When set, the line only accepts inbound INVITEs whose called number (To user-part on the registered domain) is in this list — useful to disambiguate multiple lines on the same provider domain. When null or empty, the line accepts calls for its exact username, calls delivered by the registrar it registered to, and any number on its registered domain (trunk default). Username-only accounts are unaffected. public IReadOnlyList<string>? InboundNumbers { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string> OutboundProxy Optional outbound proxy to route signaling through instead of resolving SipServer directly. public string? OutboundProxy { get; init; } Property Value string Password SIP account password. Optional: it is only needed when the registrar challenges the registration (401/407). Leave it empty for accounts that do not authenticate — for example an IP-authenticated trunk, or a registrar that does not challenge. If a challenge does arrive and no password is set, registration fails with a clear error. public string Password { get; init; } Property Value string Port Signaling port; 0 (default) selects the standard port for the chosen Transport. public int Port { get; init; } Property Value int PublicMediaHost Optional public IP address to force into the SDP media connection line (c=) and RTP bind for calls on this line. By default the media address is auto-resolved from the OS routing table and NAT is handled by symmetric RTP; set this only behind CGNAT / a static 1:1 NAT with port preservation where the peer does not latch to the source address. Must be an IP literal; non-IP values are ignored. null keeps the auto-resolved media address (default, unchanged behavior). public string? PublicMediaHost { get; init; } Property Value string PublicSipHost Optional public host (IP or FQDN) to advertise in the REGISTER Contact and Via sent-by instead of the auto-resolved local address. Required behind NAT for public SIP trunks (e.g. sipgate), whose registrar would otherwise bind the number to an unroutable private LAN address and mark the line offline. null keeps the local address (LAN/direct scenarios). public string? PublicSipHost { get; init; } Property Value string PublicSipPort Optional public signaling port paired with PublicSipHost. Use when a NAT port mapping differs from the local port. null or 0 reuses the local signaling port. public int? PublicSipPort { get; init; } Property Value int? RegistrationExpiry Requested registration lifetime in seconds; defaults to 300. public int RegistrationExpiry { get; init; } Property Value int Reregister Controls automatic re-registration when the SIP binding is lost. Defaults to Default (unlimited retries, exponential backoff). public ReregisterOptions Reregister { get; init; } Property Value ReregisterOptions SipServer SIP registrar host (IP or FQDN) and the account's SIP domain (required). public required string SipServer { get; init; } Property Value string Transport Transport used for SIP signaling; defaults to Udp. public SipTransport Transport { get; init; } Property Value SipTransport Username SIP authentication user and address user-part (required). public required string Username { get; init; } Property Value string

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Security.summary: Class SrtpDecisionReasonCodes Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Domain.Security Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Core.dll Stable reason-code constants for SRTP policy and negotiation decisions. These values are emitted in call media metadata and SIP telemetry. public static class SrtpDecisionReasonCodes Inheritance object SrtpDecisionReasonCodes Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields DisabledByPolicy SRTP policy is disabled; plain RTP was used as configured. public const string DisabledByPolicy = "srtp.disabled.by_policy" Field Value string DisabledPolicyRejectedSrtp SRTP policy is disabled but the negotiated SDP indicates secure RTP. This is a policy violation and must fail. public const string DisabledPolicyRejectedSrtp = "srtp.disabled.rejected_remote_srtp" Field Value string MediaParametersUnavailable Remote SDP could not be parsed into media parameters. public const string MediaParametersUnavailable = "srtp.media_parameters_unavailable" Field Value string Negotiated SRTP was successfully negotiated and accepted by policy. public const string Negotiated = "srtp.negotiated" Field Value string NotEvaluated No SRTP decision has been evaluated yet. public const string NotEvaluated = "srtp.not_evaluated" Field Value string OptionalFallbackToRtp SRTP policy is optional and remote negotiation ended on plain RTP. This is an explicit and allowed fallback. public const string OptionalFallbackToRtp = "srtp.optional.fallback_rtp" Field Value string RequiredNegotiationFailed SRTP policy is required but media negotiation failed under SRTP constraints. public const string RequiredNegotiationFailed = "srtp.required.negotiation_failed" Field Value string RequiredRemoteNoSrtp SRTP policy is required but no SRTP-capable remote offer/answer is available. public const string RequiredRemoteNoSrtp = "srtp.required.remote_no_srtp" Field Value string SdesKeyOverInsecureSignaling SDES SRTP is keyed over a signaling transport without confidentiality (no TLS/SIPS), so the master key travels in cleartext SDP (RFC 4568 §7). Media confidentiality is not actually assured against a passive eavesdropper on the signaling path; use TLS/SIPS or DTLS-SRTP. public const string SdesKeyOverInsecureSignaling = "srtp.sdes.insecure_signaling" Field Value string

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection..summary: Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.DependencyInjection Classes CalloraBuilder Builder for optional CalloraVoipSdk dependency overrides. CalloraHostedService Coordinates CalloraVoipSdk lifecycle through host start/stop hooks. CalloraStunServerBuilder Builder for optional STUN server-host overrides (Level 3). Returned by AddCalloraStunServer(IServiceCollection, Action<StunServerHostOptions>?). CalloraTurnServerBuilder Builder for optional TURN/STUN server-host overrides (Level 3). Returned by AddCalloraTurnServer(IServiceCollection, Action<TurnServerHostOptions>?); mirrors CalloraWebRtcBuilder. CalloraWebRtcBuilder Builder for optional WebRTC-facade dependency overrides (Level 3). Mirrors CalloraBuilder for the SIP facade and is returned by AddCalloraWebRtc(IServiceCollection, Action<WebRtcOptions>?) and by AddWebRtc(Action<WebRtcOptions>?). ServiceCollectionExtensions Dependency-injection entrypoint for CalloraVoipSdk. StunServerServiceCollectionExtensions Dependency-injection entrypoint for the STUN server host. Standalone counterpart to AddCalloraTurnServer(IServiceCollection, Action<TurnServerHostOptions>?) for a pure-STUN (Binding) deployment. TurnServerServiceCollectionExtensions Dependency-injection entrypoint for the TURN/STUN server host. Standalone counterpart to AddCalloraVoip / AddCalloraWebRtc: a host that runs a TURN server calls AddCalloraTurnServer(IServiceCollection, Action<TurnServerHostOptions>?) and receives a builder for optional overrides. VoipOptionsValidator Startup validation for host-bound VoipOptions. WebRtcServiceCollectionExtensions Dependency-injection entrypoint for the WebRTC facade. Standalone counterpart to AddCalloraVoip(IServiceCollection, Action<VoipOptions>?): a pure-WebRTC host calls only AddCalloraWebRtc(IServiceCollection, Action<WebRtcOptions>?); a host that wants both facades chains AddCalloraVoip(...).AddWebRtc(...) (ADR-012, two-facade composition).

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.IStunServerH.title: Interface IStunServerHost | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.IStunServerH.summary: Interface IStunServerHost Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll A hostable STUN server (RFC 5389) for server-reflexive address discovery — the pure-STUN counterpart to ITurnServerHost. It binds its socket on construction (so LocalEndPoint is known immediately, including after an ephemeral bind), answers Binding requests from Start(), and releases everything on disposal. public interface IStunServerHost : IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members IAsyncDisposable.DisposeAsync() Properties LocalEndPoint The endpoint the server is bound to (the actual port after an ephemeral :0 bind). IPEndPoint LocalEndPoint { get; } Property Value IPEndPoint Methods Start() Starts answering STUN Binding requests. Idempotent — a second call, or a call after disposal, is a no-op. void Start()

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.ITurnServerH.title: Interface ITurnServerHost | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.ITurnServerH.summary: Interface ITurnServerHost Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll A hostable TURN relay server (RFC 8656) — the server-side counterpart to the client facades (VoipClient, WebRtcClient). It binds its socket on construction (so LocalEndPoint is known immediately, including after an ephemeral bind), starts serving on Start(), and stops and releases everything on disposal. It serves the TURN methods (Allocate / Refresh / CreatePermission / ChannelBind / Send / Data, plus the TCP extension); it does not answer bare STUN Binding requests — for server-reflexive discovery run an IStunServerHost alongside it. public interface ITurnServerHost : IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members IAsyncDisposable.DisposeAsync() Properties LocalEndPoint The endpoint the server is bound to (the actual port after an ephemeral :0 bind). IPEndPoint LocalEndPoint { get; } Property Value IPEndPoint Methods Start() Starts the server's listen loop. Idempotent — a second call, or a call after disposal, is a no-op. void Start()

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting.StunServerHo.summary: Class StunServerHost Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.Hosting Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll The public STUN server host (Level 2 facade). Assembles the internal CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Infrastructure.Stun.Server.StunServer from a StunServerHostConfiguration — binding the socket on construction — and answers Binding requests with a CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Infrastructure.Stun.Server.StunBindingRequestHandler once started. Kept in the Client layer so the server infrastructure stays an internal implementation detail reached only through this facade. public sealed class StunServerHost : IStunServerHost, IAsyncDisposable Inheritance object StunServerHost Implements IStunServerHost IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors StunServerHost(StunServerHostConfiguration) Builds and binds the server from configuration. The socket is bound here, so LocalEndPoint is valid before Start(). public StunServerHost(StunServerHostConfiguration configuration) Parameters configuration StunServerHostConfiguration Exceptions ArgumentException A TLS transport without a certificate. Properties LocalEndPoint The endpoint the server is bound to (the actual port after an ephemeral :0 bind). public IPEndPoint LocalEndPoint { get; } Property Value IPEndPoint Methods DisposeAsync() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources asynchronously. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask A task that represents the asynchronous dispose operation. Start() Starts answering STUN Binding requests. Idempotent — a second call, or a call after disposal, is a no-op. public void Start()

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.SipTransport.html.title: Enum SipTransport | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.SipTransport.html.summary: Enum SipTransport Namespace CalloraVoipSdk Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll Default SIP signaling transport a consumer selects via DefaultTransport. Determines which transport the SDK uses for outbound requests and its advertised local contact when a target URI does not force a transport (for example via a sips: scheme or ;transport= parameter). All five transports are listened on regardless; this only selects the default for outbound routing. public enum SipTransport Fields Tcp = 1 SIP over TCP (RFC 3261). Tls = 2 SIP over TLS / SIPS (RFC 3261 §26.2). Udp = 0 SIP over UDP (RFC 3261). The default. Ws = 3 SIP over WebSocket (RFC 7118). Wss = 4 SIP over secure WebSocket (RFC 7118).

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.VoipConfiguration.ht.title: Class VoipConfiguration | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.VoipConfiguration.ht.summary: Class VoipConfiguration Namespace CalloraVoipSdk Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll Top-level SDK configuration: identity, transport security, logging, media/codec behavior, and call-lifecycle timeouts. Supplied once when constructing or registering the SDK. public sealed class VoipConfiguration Inheritance object VoipConfiguration Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties AudioDevice Audio device to use for all calls. If left at SilenceAudioDevice and auto selection is enabled, the SDK attempts to load a platform device (Linux/Windows) at runtime. public IAudioDevice AudioDevice { get; init; } Property Value IAudioDevice BridgeAudioFormat Audio format delivered to and expected from the media consumer (bridge/tap). When set to Pcmu, the SDK transcodes between the negotiated wire codec (e.g. Opus) and G.711 µ-law so a µ-law-only consumer works over any negotiated codec. Default Passthrough delivers the raw wire payload. public BridgeAudioFormat BridgeAudioFormat { get; init; } Property Value BridgeAudioFormat DefaultTransport Default SIP signaling transport for outbound requests and the advertised local contact when a target URI does not force one. Defaults to Udp, preserving the prior behavior; set to Tcp/Tls/etc. for TCP- or TLS-only enterprise proxies. public SipTransport DefaultTransport { get; init; } Property Value SipTransport DtlsCertificate Optional DTLS-SRTP identity certificate (RFC 5763) for the media plane. null (default) generates a fresh ephemeral ECDSA P-256 certificate per client instance — the WebRTC privacy default. Supply your own for a stable/pinned identity (enterprise, compliance): it must be an ECDSA P-256 certificate with an accessible private key (RSA, other curves, and non-exportable HSM keys are rejected fail-closed). The DTLS certificate is authenticated by SDP a=fingerprint (RFC 8122), not PKI, and is independent of the SIP-TLS certificate (Tls) — pass the same X509Certificate2 to both to share one identity. public X509Certificate2? DtlsCertificate { get; init; } Property Value X509Certificate2 EnableAutomaticAudioDeviceSelection Automatically load a platform audio device when AudioDevice is left at CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Infrastructure.Audio.SilenceAudioDevice. public bool EnableAutomaticAudioDeviceSelection { get; init; } Property Value bool EnableVideo When true, calls negotiate a video stream (WebRTC phase 2, RFC 6184/7741): offers carry an m=video line and inbound video offers are answered. Encoded video frames are exchanged via ICall's media session; the SDK does not encode/decode video itself. Default: false (audio-only). Video is offered only when the offer is not SDES-keyed: an outbound offer under a SDES-offering SrtpPolicy (Optional/Required without OfferDtlsSrtp) stays audio-only — offer DTLS-SRTP or run plain to carry video. Inbound plain/DTLS video offers are answered regardless; SDES video is declined until per-m-line video keying lands. Note: video does not yet gather ICE candidates — with Ice enabled the video m-line carries its port but no candidates, so video needs direct connectivity (no ICE-only peer) until per-component video ICE lands. public bool EnableVideo { get; init; } Property Value bool HangupHeldCallOnMediaSilence Whether the inbound-media timeout also applies to on-hold calls (which legitimately carry no inbound RTP). Default: false (held calls are not torn down). public bool HangupHeldCallOnMediaSilence { get; init; } Property Value bool Ice ICE runtime configuration for NAT traversal and candidate-pair selection. Disabled by default. public IceConfiguration Ice { get; init; } Property Value IceConfiguration InboundMediaTimeout Hang up a connected call whose inbound RTP has been silent this long — a NAT-safe fallback for when a far-end BYE never reaches our in-dialog Contact and the media just stops. Zero disables the check. Default: 15 seconds. public TimeSpan InboundMediaTimeout { get; init; } Property Value TimeSpan LoggerFactory Logger factory the SDK logs through; null disables SDK logging. public ILoggerFactory? LoggerFactory { get; init; } Property Value ILoggerFactory MaxConcurrentCallsPerLine Maximum simultaneous calls per phone line. 0 = unlimited. public int MaxConcurrentCallsPerLine { get; init; } Property Value int OfferDtlsSrtp When true, outbound call offers advertise DTLS-SRTP keying (RFC 5763: UDP/TLS/RTP/SAVPF profile plus certificate fingerprint) instead of SDES a=crypto. Inbound DTLS-SRTP offers are answered regardless of this setting. Default: false (SDES per SrtpPolicy). public bool OfferDtlsSrtp { get; init; } Property Value bool PreferredAudioCodecs Ordered audio codec preference by SDP encoding name ("PCMU", "PCMA", "G722", "opus"). When set, SDP offers and answers only include the listed codecs (plus DTMF telephone-event) in this order, and RTP sessions use this preference to pick the primary codec. Opus (RFC 7587, 48 kHz) is opt-in: it is only offered/answered when listed here. Unknown names are ignored; when nothing matches, the SDK default set (G722, PCMA, PCMU) is used. When a listed codec is known but the peer does not offer it, negotiation fails rather than producing an audio-less answer. null keeps defaults. public IReadOnlyList<string>? PreferredAudioCodecs { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string> PreferredVideoCodecs Ordered video codec preference by SDP encoding name (VP8, H264) when EnableVideo is set. null uses the SDK default (VP8, then H264). Unknown names are ignored. public IReadOnlyList<string>? PreferredVideoCodecs { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string> RequireSecureSignalingForSdes Opt-in hard enforcement of the RFC 4568 §7 caveat (see SrtpPolicy). When true, an outbound call that would key SDES over an insecure signaling transport (no TLS/SIPS, and OfferDtlsSrtp unset) is refused (fail-closed) instead of placed with the master key in cleartext SDP. Default: false — such calls proceed but the SDK logs a warning. Set this when a non-confidential media key must never leave the host; use TLS/SIPS signaling or DTLS-SRTP to place calls under this policy. public bool RequireSecureSignalingForSdes { get; init; } Property Value bool SrtpPolicy Default media-encryption policy for calls; defaults to Optional. Overridable per call via DialOptions.UseSrtp. Security note (RFC 4568 §7): unless OfferDtlsSrtp is set, SRTP is keyed via SDES, which carries the master key as an a=crypto line inside the SDP. That key is only confidential when the signaling transport is secure (TLS/SIPS). Over UDP/TCP the key travels in cleartext, so a passive eavesdropper on the signaling path can decrypt the media — the SDK logs a warning in this case. For real media confidentiality use TLS/SIPS signaling or enable OfferDtlsSrtp (keys never appear in the SDP). public SrtpPolicy SrtpPolicy { get; init; } Property Value SrtpPolicy Tls TLS settings for secure SIP transport; null uses the transport defaults. public TlsConfiguration? Tls { get; init; } Property Value TlsConfiguration UserAgent Value sent in the SIP User-Agent header; defaults to CalloraVoipSdk/1.0. public string UserAgent { get; init; } Property Value string

DurationMs int The reassembled tone duration in milliseconds. Valid for the duration of the FrameReceived callback. public ReadOnlyMemory<byte> Payload { get; } Property Value ReadOnlyMemory<byte> PresentationTimeUsec The wall-clock presentation time in microseconds for lip-sync across tracks, when known; null until the RTCP-SR RTP↔NTP mapping lands (ADR-012 deferred item). public long? _api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IEncodedMedia.title: Interface IEncodedMediaSink | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IEncodedMedia.summary: Interface IEncodedMediaSink Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll A destination for recorded encoded media. The recorder writes every captured RecordedFrame here (both directions, audio and video); the sink decides how to persist it — a per-track file, a container, an upload, an in-memory buffer, … Transport-only: payloads are the raw codec bitstream (no decoding). A container writer (Opus→Ogg, VP8→IVF, muxed MP4/…) is a concrete sink implementation. public interface IEncodedMediaSink Remarks Write(in RecordedFrame) is invoked on the media path — it must return quickly and should not throw (a throwing sink is isolated from the media flow and its frame is dropped). CompleteAsync(CancellationToken) is invoked exactly once when the recording stops, to flush and finalise. Methods CompleteAsync(CancellationToken) Flushes and finalises the sink; called once when recording stops. ValueTask CompleteAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns ValueTask Write(in RecordedFrame) Persists one captured frame. void Write(in RecordedFrame frame) Parameters frame RecordedFrame

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IMediaTap.htm.title: Interface IMediaTap | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IMediaTap.htm.summary: Interface IMediaTap Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll An L3 observer of the encoded media flowing through an IPeerConnection in both directions — the extension seam for recording, analytics, or feeding an AI pipeline without owning the peer. Attach with AttachMediaTap(IMediaTap) and dispose the handle to detach. public interface IMediaTap Remarks Transport-only: payloads are the raw depacketised codec bitstream. A tap is invoked on the media path, so it must return quickly and should not throw — a throwing tap is caught, logged, and isolated from the media flow rather than being allowed to break it. Methods OnAudio(MediaDirection, ReadOnlyMemory<byte>) Observes one audio payload in the given direction. void OnAudio(MediaDirection direction, ReadOnlyMemory<byte> payload) Parameters direction MediaDirection payload ReadOnlyMemory<byte> OnVideo(MediaDirection, ReadOnlyMemory<byte>, uint?, bool, string?) Observes one video frame in the given direction. rtpTimestamp is null when unknown; isKeyFrame is false for outbound frames (the send path does not carry the flag). rid is the simulcast layer id (RFC 8853) for an outbound simulcast frame, so a tap can tell the layers apart; it is null for a single-stream send and for inbound frames (receive-side RID demux is a later slice). Richer per-frame metadata (MID/SSRC/track ids) will follow when those are surfaced. void OnVideo(MediaDirection direction, ReadOnlyMemory<byte> frame, uint? rtpTimestamp, bool isKeyFrame, string? rid) Parameters direction MediaDirection frame ReadOnlyMemory<byte> rtpTimestamp uint? isKeyFrame bool rid string

_api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IPeerConnecti.title: Interface IPeerConnection | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IPeerConnecti.summary: Interface IPeerConnection Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll A signalling-neutral WebRTC peer connection: the app owns the signalling channel (WebSocket/HTTP/ Callora) and exchanges SDP through it, while this peer runs ICE, DTLS-SRTP, BUNDLE and the RTP/RTCP transport. Encoded media flows through SendAudioAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte>, CancellationToken)/SendVideoFrameAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte>, uint, CancellationToken) — the SDK is transport-only, so the app owns the codec. The central per-connection abstraction of the WebRTC facade, mirroring the SIP ICall. public interface IPeerConnection : IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members IAsyncDisposable.DisposeAsync() Extension Methods WebRtcPeerConnectionExtensions.ConnectAsync(IPeerConnection, IWebRtcSignaling, WebRtcRole, CancellationToken) Properties LocalDescription The local SDP (offer or answer) once one has been produced; null before. string? LocalDescription { get; } Property Value string LocalMediaEndPoint The bound local media endpoint once the transport has bound; null before. IPEndPoint? LocalMediaEndPoint { get; } Property Value IPEndPoint State Current lifecycle state (RFC 8829). PeerConnectionState State { get; } Property Value PeerConnectionState Methods AddIceCandidateAsync(string, CancellationToken) Applies a remote ICE candidate that trickled in out-of-band (RFC 8838), as an RFC 8829 candidate: line. The highest-priority component-1 UDP candidate becomes the send target; a malformed or unusable candidate is ignored. Task AddIceCandidateAsync(string candidate, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters candidate string cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task AttachMediaTap(IMediaTap) Attaches an IMediaTap that observes the encoded media flowing through this peer in both directions (L3 recording/analytics/AI seam). Dispose the returned handle to detach. IDisposable AttachMediaTap(IMediaTap tap) Parameters tap IMediaTap Returns IDisposable CreateOffer() Produces a local WebRTC offer (BUNDLE, DTLS-SRTP, ICE, rtcp-mux) for the app to signal out. string CreateOffer() Returns string GatherCandidatesAsync(CancellationToken) Gathers server-reflexive ICE candidates (RFC 8445 §5.1.1) from the configured STUN servers, each surfaced on LocalIceCandidateDiscovered to trickle out (RFC 8838). No-op when no STUN servers are configured. Call after producing the offer/answer and before StartAsync(CancellationToken) (the query shares the media socket the transport takes over once started; calling it after StartAsync(CancellationToken) throws). On loopback (the STUN server on the same host) the reflexive address equals the host candidate; redundant-candidate pruning (RFC 8445 §5.4) is a later slice. Task GatherCandidatesAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task GetStats() Takes a statistics snapshot for this peer (the SDK's getStats). Bitrates are derived per call, so poll periodically (e.g. once per second) for meaningful rate values. WebRtcStats GetStats() Returns WebRtcStats SendAudioAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte>, CancellationToken) Sends one already-encoded audio RTP payload on the peer's audio track. A no-op when the negotiated directions do not carry outbound audio from this peer (a send-only/inactive remote answer, or a recv-only/inactive local side, RFC 3264): the audio m-line still anchors the transport and inbound audio is still received, but nothing is streamed to a remote that will not receive it. ValueTask SendAudioAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte> payload, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters payload ReadOnlyMemory<byte> cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns ValueTask SendDtmfAsync(byte, int, CancellationToken) Sends one out-of-band DTMF tone (RFC 4733 telephone-event) on the peer's audio track. A no-op is not possible: telephone-event must have been negotiated, otherwise this throws. The tone is streamed as an event burst on the audio stream's RTP clock, suppressed until the DTLS handshake keys the transport. Task SendDtmfAsync(byte toneCode, int durationMs = 160, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters toneCode byte The DTMF event code (0–9, 10=*, 11=#, 12–15=A–D per RFC 4733 §3.2). durationMs int The tone duration in milliseconds (default 160; at least the RFC 4733 floor). cancellationToken CancellationToken Cancels the send. Returns Task Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException The tone code exceeds 15, or the duration is below the floor. InvalidOperationException No media session yet, or telephone-event was not negotiated. SendVideoFrameAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte>, uint, CancellationToken) Packetises and sends one already-encoded video frame on the peer's video track. Task SendVideoFrameAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte> encodedFrame, uint rtpTimestamp, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters encodedFrame ReadOnlyMemory<byte> rtpTimestamp uint cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task SendVideoFrameAsync(string, ReadOnlyMemory<byte>, uint, CancellationToken) Packetises and sends one already-encoded video frame on a simulcast rid layer (RFC 8853). The layer must be one of the configured simulcast rids; the app encodes each layer at its own resolution/bitrate and calls this once per layer per frame. Task SendVideoFrameAsync(string rid, ReadOnlyMemory<byte> encodedFrame, uint rtpTimestamp, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters rid string encodedFrame ReadOnlyMemory<byte> rtpTimestamp uint cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task SetRemoteDescriptionAsync(string, CancellationToken) Applies a remote SDP. When this peer is the answerer, returns the local answer SDP to signal back; when it is the offerer applying the peer's answer, returns the local offer unchanged. ConnectionStateChanged Event Type EventHandler<PeerConnectionState> DtmfReceived Raised once per fully received inbound DTMF tone (RFC 4733 telephone-event). Carries the decoded tone and duration; DTMF is not surfaced as audio on the remote audio track. Only fires when the negotiation included telephone-event. event EventHandler<DtmfTone>? DtmfReceived Event Type EventHandler<DtmfTone> LocalIceCandidateDiscovered Raised as each local ICE candidate is gathered (RFC 8838 trickle), carrying the RFC 8829 candidate: line so the app can signal it to the peer out-of-band. _api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcSignal.summary: Interface IWebRtcSignaling Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll The app-owned signalling channel the SDK drives during ConnectAsync(IPeerConnection, IWebRtcSignaling, WebRtcRole, CancellationToken): a bidirectional transport (WebSocket, HTTP long-poll, Callora signalling, …) that carries SDP descriptions between the two peers. The SDK stays signalling-neutral — it only sends and receives SDP through this contract; the app decides how the bytes travel. public interface IWebRtcSignaling Methods ReceiveDescriptionAsync(CancellationToken) Awaits the next SDP description (answer or offer) from the remote peer on the app's channel. _api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcTrickl.title: Interface IWebRtcTrickleSignaling | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.IWebRtcTrickl.summary: Interface IWebRtcTrickleSignaling Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll Extends IWebRtcSignaling with out-of-band ICE candidate trickle (RFC 8838): alongside the offer/answer, the SDK sends each locally gathered candidate as it is discovered and applies remote candidates as they arrive, rather than relying only on the candidates the SDP carried. When an app supplies this on ConnectAsync(IPeerConnection, IWebRtcSignaling, WebRtcRole, CancellationToken), the SDK gathers server-reflexive candidates and runs the trickle exchange; a plain IWebRtcSignaling stays SDP-only. Candidate strings are RFC 8829 candidate:… lines — the wire form browsers exchange verbatim, so no conversion is needed at the app boundary. public interface IWebRtcTrickleSignaling : IWebRtcSignaling Inherited Members IWebRtcSignaling.SendDescriptionAsync(string, CancellationToken) IWebRtcSignaling.ReceiveDescriptionAsync(CancellationToken) Methods ReceiveCandidateAsync(CancellationToken) Awaits the next remote ICE candidate line, or null when the remote has signalled end-of-candidates (RFC 8840) — after which the SDK stops polling for more. Task<string?> ReceiveCandidateAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task<string> SendCandidateAsync(string, CancellationToken) Sends one locally gathered ICE candidate (an RFC 8829 candidate: line) to the remote peer over the app's channel. Task SendCandidateAsync(string candidate, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters candidate string cancellationToken CancellationToken Returns Task SendEndOfCandidatesAsync(CancellationToken) Signals that local gathering is complete and no further local candidates will be sent (RFC 8840 a=end-of-candidates semantics, carried out-of-band). _api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.PeerConnectio.title: Enum PeerConnectionState | CalloraVoipSdk
_api/CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc.PeerConnectio.summary: Enum PeerConnectionState Namespace CalloraVoipSdk.WebRtc Assembly CalloraVoipSdk.Client.dll Lifecycle state of a WebRTC IPeerConnection, modelled on the W3C RTCPeerConnectionState (RFC 8829): a signalling-neutral view of where the peer is between creation and teardown. ICE consent and the DTLS handshake drive the transitions. public enum PeerConnectionState Fields Closed = 5 The peer was closed and its resources released. Connected = 2 ICE consent and the DTLS handshake are established; media can flow. Connecting = 1 A remote description was applied and the transport is being established (ICE/DTLS). Disconnected = 3 Connectivity was lost transiently (RFC 7675 consent miss) but may recover. Failed = 4 Negotiation or the transport failed unrecoverably. New = 0 Created; no remote description applied yet.
